title: Run a checkpoint recovery drill
description: Steps to force a failure, recover from a checkpoint, and reconcile output for loss and duplication.
body: Use when validating that a stateful job recovers cleanly. Do not assume checkpointing works because it is enabled — only a drill proves it. Actions: feed a counted dataset, kill a task mid-stream to trigger checkpoint recovery, let the job resume, and reconcile the sink against the input for both missing and duplicated records. Validation signals include a recovered output count exactly matching input and a sink log showing replays were deduplicated or transactionally committed. Risk boundary: undetected duplication on recovery corrupts downstream aggregates; if the drill reveals loss or duplicates, treat the job as not production-ready and escalate to fix the sink or state handling.
